data.title: Cathie Wood takes first stake in Alibaba in four years, worth over $16 million
data.description: Cathie Wood, known as "Wood Sister," has reopened her Alibaba (BABA.N) fund for the first time in four years. According to the daily trading report released by Wood's Ark Investment Management, two of its ETFs purchased Alibaba ADRs on Monday, totaling approximately $16.3 million. On Tuesday, Alibaba ADRs hit their highest level since November 2021, nearly doubling year-to-date. According to the SEC's search tool, Ark's first investment in Alibaba dates back to 2014, shortly after the company went public that year. However, no records of investments or proxy voting have been found since September 2021. This latest investment may signal a renewed expansion of Ark's investments in the Chinese internet sector.

data.title: The US CFTC launches a tokenized collateral program to allow the use of stablecoins in derivatives transactions
data.description: Acting Chairwoman Caroline D. Pham of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) announced the launch of a "tokenized collateral" initiative, allowing derivatives traders to use non-cash assets such as stablecoins as collateral to improve market efficiency and transparency. This initiative builds on a pilot program launched in February of this year with Circle, Coinbase, Crypto.com, Ripple, and Moonpay. The industry is invited to submit comments by October 20th. This move is seen as a significant step forward by the CFTC in modernizing capital markets and clarifying crypto regulation.

data.title: Fold partners with Stripe and Visa to launch Bitcoin rewards credit card
data.description: Nasdaq-listed company Fold (FLD) announced a partnership with Stripe and Visa to launch a Bitcoin rewards credit card. Running on the Visa network, users will receive 2% instant Bitcoin cash back on every purchase, increasing to 3.5% when paying with a Fold checking account and up to 10% at select participating retailers. The card requires no collateral or exchange account, and rewards are automatically distributed in Bitcoin.

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in October is 93%.
data.description: According to CME's "Fed Watch," the probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in October is 7%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 93%. The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in December is 1.3%, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 22.5%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut is 76.2%.

data.title: Solana Infrastructure Project Raiku Secures $13.5 Million in Funding
data.description: Blockchain startup Raiku announced the completion of $13.5 million in seed and pre-seed funding rounds. The seed round was led by Pantera Capital, with participation from Jump Crypto, Lightspeed Faction, and others, while the pre-seed round was led by Figment Capital and Big Brain Holdings, with participation from Reciprocal Ventures and Anagram. Raiku aims to provide a predictable and guaranteed transaction execution environment for the Solana network by building blockspace coordination infrastructure. Raiku founder Robin Nordnes noted that while Solana is known for its high speed, its reliability significantly decreases during periods of network congestion. Raiku's parallel execution layer and blockspace reservation mechanism enable developers and institutions to pre-confirm transactions with microsecond accuracy, ensuring reliable execution even under extreme loads. The Raiku testnet is now live, with a mainnet launch planned for 2026. The funding will be used to accelerate technology development and ecosystem expansion.

data.title: SEC and CFTC Collaborate on Market Structure Legislation
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul S. Atkins announced on the program 'Mornings with Maria' that the SEC is working closely with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to advance market structure legislation. This collaboration includes joint roundtable discussions and future joint rule-making efforts.

Atkins emphasized the need for a clear regulatory framework for the crypto asset market and suggested introducing innovative exemptions to support the development of on-chain capital markets. Additionally, the SEC plans to review initial public offering (IPO) reporting requirements and explore diverse opportunities for retail investors to participate in the private market.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses Fusaka Upgrade and Blockchain Efficiency
data.description: According to PANews, Vitalik Buterin has outlined the core features of the Fusaka upgrade, focusing on the PeerDAS system, which aims to enhance blockchain operations without requiring single nodes to download complete data sets. PeerDAS utilizes node sampling to verify block data availability and employs erasure codes to recover missing data. The initial version will still require complete data during the first broadcast and reconstruction, but only one honest participant is necessary. Future developments will include distributed messaging and block construction. Buterin emphasized the importance of security, noting that the number of blobs will gradually increase to facilitate Layer 2 and Layer 1 scaling.

data.title: Bitcoin’s Path Unclear As Subwave Chaos Meets Key Resistance At $113,000
data.description:
Bitcoin’s price action is caught in uncertainty as messy subwave structures clash with a critical resistance at $113,000. While the market shows attempts at recovery, the unclear wave patterns leave traders divided on whether the next move will be a breakout or a deeper correction. Messy Subwaves Keep Bitcoin’s Next Move Clouded TARA, a crypto analyst, recently shared fresh insights on X regarding Bitcoin’s ongoing price action. According to the analyst, Bitcoin is currently in the middle of forming another wave down, but the subwave structure is still messy and unclear
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoins-path-unclear/

data.title: Bitcoin Price Rises as Gold Pulls Back — Is BTC About to Take the Lead?
data.description:
The recent surge in Bitcoin prices has caught the attention of investors as gold experiences a pullback in its value. This shift has led many to wonder if Bitcoin, the pioneering cryptocurrency, is poised to take the lead in the financial markets, capitalizing on the opportunity presented by gold's decline. As traditional safe-haven assets face challenges, will Bitcoin emerge as the preferred choice for those seeking stability and growth?
source: https://blocknews.com/bitcoin-price-rises-as-gold-pulls-back-is-btc-about-to-take-the-lead/
